description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with a natural brown finish and a visibly ergonomic grip. The axe head is forged from dark gray metal, with a broad, single-edged blade that tapers to a sharp point and a flat poll on the opposite side. The blade has a distinct, angular profile with a beveled cutting edge and a subtle beard extending downward for improved chopping efficiency. The handle appears to be securely fitted into the eye of the axe head, suggesting a traditional wedged mounting style. There are no visible decorative elements, engravings, or branding on either the handle or the blade. The overall design is utilitarian, suitable for tasks such as chopping wood or clearing brush in urban parks, gardens, or maintenance areas.
description: A utilitarian hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad, single-edged metal blade. The design is simple and functional, with no visible markings or decorations.
description_view_0: The axe is shown from a three-quarter angle, highlighting the curved wooden handle and the broad, angular blade with a sharp edge.
description_view_1: This top-down view emphasizes the length and curvature of the handle, as well as the thickness of the axe head.
description_view_2: A side view from above, showing the profile of the blade and the handle's attachment to the head.
description_view_3: A direct side view clearly displays the full blade shape, the beard, and the ergonomic curve of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with an ergonomic grip, oval cross-section, and a natural finish.
attribute_blade_design: Single-edged, broad, angular steel blade with a beveled edge and a subtle beard; flat poll on the opposite side.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the handle fitted through the eye of the blade.
